complete_message: Understanding your patterns is the first step toward rewriting them. You carry the power to choose a different path.

screen1.intro: Ever felt like you've been here before?
screen1.title: If your relationships keep ending the same way — it's not bad luck.
screen1.desc: The relationships we choose follow a blueprint written long before we ever fell in love. Familiarity often feels like safety, even when it leads to the same old arguments.
screen1.quote: "We repeat patterns not because we are broken — but because they feel familiar."

screen2.intro: It starts earlier than you think.
screen2.title: Earliest relationships become the template for all that follows.
screen2.desc: The way your caregivers responded to your needs quietly shaped how you relate to others as an adult. These are learned responses — and they can be unlearned.
screen2.quote: "Your pattern made sense once. The question is — does it still serve you now?"

screen3.intro: Why do we keep dancing to the same tune?
screen3.title: The Dance of Pursuer and Distancer.
screen3.desc: Often, one partner pulls away when feeling overwhelmed, while the other leans in out of anxiety. Understanding this dynamic is key to breaking the loop.
screen3.quote: "True connection requires us to step out of the dance and hear the music."

screen4.intro: It's not what you say, it's how you hear it.
screen4.title: We often listen to reply, not to understand.
screen4.desc: When conflicts arise, our defenses go up. Recognizing when you're reacting defensively can transform arguments into opportunities for intimacy.
screen4.quote: "Vulnerability is the only bridge to genuine understanding."

screen5.title: Awareness is the first step of change.
screen5.desc: You don't have to be a victim of your patterns. By identifying triggers and pausing before reacting, you can consciously choose a new response.
screen5.quote: "Between stimulus and response there is a space. In that space is our power to choose our response."

common.completion_message: You've successfully completed this activity. Take a moment to appreciate your progress.
common.share_text: I have done this {{activityName}} in TherapyMantra and really enjoy it you can do it too just follow the link to android app {{androidUrl}} or ios app {{iosUrl}}
